# orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource (Data Source)

Looks up an existing ServiceNow credentials resource (the entity managed by the
[`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource`](../resources/integration_servicenow_resource.md)
resource) by its human-friendly `name`. The same credentials back both ITSM and
SIR templates.

Common reasons to use this data source instead of creating the resource:

* Credentials were created in the Orca UI and you want to attach Terraform-managed
  templates to them.
* Credentials live in a different Terraform workspace.
* The ServiceNow account or instance is not reachable from the workspace running
  Terraform, so the create-time connectivity check on the resource would fail
  (`Invalid credentials or ServiceNow instance unavailable.`).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing ServiceNow credentials resource by name and feed its id into a
# template — useful when the credentials were created via the UI or a different Terraform
# workspace. The same credentials resource backs both ITSM and SIR templates.
data "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource" "existing" {
  name = "servicenow-prod"
}

resource "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template" "demo" {
  template_name = "Yuri Demo"
  resource_id   = data.orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource.existing.id
  instance_name = "ven03666"

  resolution_status = "6"
  resolution_code   = "Resolved by caller"
  resolution_note   = "Resolved by Yuri"

  mapping_json = jsonencode({
    category    = [{ value = "software" }]
    u_risk_name = [{ orca = "alert_name" }]
  })
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `name` — (Required, String) Human-friendly name of the existing ServiceNow
  credentials resource. The data source fails if zero or more than one resource
  match.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca resource identifier (UUID). Feed this into the
  `resource_id` argument on `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template`
  or `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_template`.
* `servicenow_url` — (String) ServiceNow instance URL stored on the resource.
* `username` — (String) ServiceNow username stored on the resource.
